---
title: "10-Week Unit Plan"
---

## Target Standard

**AS92005 — Develop a digital technologies outcome (Level 1, Internal, 5 Credits)**

## Phase 1: The Quarto Paradigm (Weeks 1–2)

### Objective
Understand how Quarto differs from drag-and-drop or raw HTML workflows.

### Topics

- Installing and navigating RStudio or VS Code
- Markdown basics (headings, lists, emphasis, links)
- Static site generator concept (source files render to HTML)

### Activity
Create a one-page `About Me` file and render it to HTML.

## Phase 2: Structuring the Website (Weeks 3–4)

### Objective

Build a multi-page website using configuration files.

### Topics

- Creating a Quarto website project
- Understanding `_quarto.yml`
- Building navigation bars and sidebars
- Applying a Bootswatch theme for styling

### Activity

Create website skeleton with blank pages and working navigation.

## Phase 3: Integrating Data with R (Weeks 5–6)

### Objective

Embed computational content in the website.

### Topics

- R code chunks in `.qmd` files
- Loading environmental CSV data in R
- Basic data checks and summary calculations
- Creating one clear environmental chart

### Activity

Choose either `data/environment_nz.csv` or `data/waste_recycling_nz.csv`, then add at least one working chart with a short interpretation.

## Phase 4: Development and Iteration (Weeks 7–8)

### Objective
Develop full project content and improve quality.

### Topics

- Building complete page content
- Functional testing (links, rendering, readability)
- Peer testing for usability feedback and scientific clarity

### Activity
Independent development with documented changes and evidence linked to Achieved/Merit/Excellence.

## Phase 5: Deployment and Submission (Weeks 9–10)

### Objective
Publish and submit complete evidence.

### Topics

- Final render
- Publish with Quarto Pub or GitHub Pages
- Brief evaluation against intended outcome

### Activity
Submit live URL, source files, and testing/evaluation evidence.
